title: Defending Against AI-Enabled Data Fusion
gist: Multimodal AI data fusion lets attackers build detailed digital twins of people and manipulate model outputs, shifting the security battleground from data confidentiality to data integrity and requiring hardware roots of trust, isolation, and authenticated data movement.

insights.claim: By fusing white-, gray-, and black-market data, attackers can construct a detailed digital twin of a person and their environment, making targeted attacks far easier.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Data fusion turns scattered leaks into weaponizable profiles, raising the stakes for privacy tech and secure-by-design silicon.
insights.quote: you can fuse all that data into a profile or a digital twin of a person
insights.locator: There’s a lot of white market

insights.claim: Once attackers infiltrate a home via routers, PCs, phones, or wearables, they can continuously harvest location and behavioral data, and controlling the phone is worst because it controls all connected devices.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: The phone as central hub is the highest-value target; endpoint and device-trust security is the key defensive layer.
insights.quote: If they can get into your phone, that’s even worse, because then it’s all the devices connected to and controlled by your phone.

insights.claim: GPS-attached wearable data tied to a person guarantees total loss of privacy, illustrated by hackers using fitness-watch data to track soldiers marching around building perimeters before anonymization.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Location-linked wearable data is a concrete national-security and privacy vector, strengthening the case for on-device anonymization.
insights.quote: once you have a GPS and GPS-attached data, and you can connect that to a person, then they are 100% guaranteed to lose any semblance of privacy
insights.locator: The personal wearable IoT is especially problematic

themes.theme: AI shifts the target from data to the model

insights.claim: AI itself is the attack vector, not the individual data streams, so the key defense is to block manipulation of the fusion layer rather than control each raw data source.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Reframes defense strategy: protect the fusion/inference layer, not just encrypt individual feeds — a design-priority shift.
insights.quote: AI itself is actually the attack vector, not the individual data streams
insights.locator: It’s how you lay the foundation

insights.claim: AI data fusion binds individual streams into local intelligence, so attackers who can influence the output no longer need to steal the raw data, dramatically enlarging the runtime attack surface beyond conventional secure-boot approaches.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Runtime inference security (memory, compute paths) becomes the new frontier — secure boot alone is insufficient.
insights.quote: if they can influence the output, they don’t need to steal the data
insights.locator: AI has fundamentally changed data fusion

insights.claim: Integrity attacks have become more dangerous than simple data theft because glitching or manipulating fused input can corrupt inference and make the model useless or dangerous, yet the industry underinvests in integrity because it is a harder memory-side problem.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Data integrity is an under-served security category relative to confidentiality — a gap and opportunity for integrity-protection IP.
insights.quote: integrity attacks have become much more dangerous than simple data theft
insights.locator: The challenge here is understanding the combinatorial risk

insights.claim: AI data fusion amplifies risk by combining models from semi-independent data sources never designed to trust each other, making it hard to predict how a model responds if one source injects malicious data unlike its training data.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Black-box interaction risk between disparate systems means threat modeling must cover unknown cross-system failure modes.
insights.quote: you’re attempting to protect against previously unknown, black-box interactions between many disparate systems
insights.locator: The big issue with AI data fusion

themes.theme: Hardware root of trust as foundation

insights.claim: Defending fused data requires a chain of trust starting in silicon — hardware root of trust, system-level attestation, and authenticated supply-chain components — where the chip enforces trust and never assumes it.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Silicon-anchored trust is the mandatory foundation, favoring root-of-trust and attestation IP vendors like Rambus and Arm.
insights.quote: The chip must enforce trust and never assume it.
insights.locator: AI data fusion requires enforcing trust

insights.claim: Cryptographically verifying and authenticating every data source before it feeds an inference operation shrinks the exploitable attack surface, requiring tamper-resistant hardware to transform untrusted software into something trustworthy.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Per-source authentication before inference is the concrete design pattern; software without a hardware anchor is an insecure foundation.
insights.quote: Without a structured, specific path to trusted hardware, everything executing in software is built on an insecure foundation.
insights.locator: That starts with a hardware root of trust

insights.claim: Arm's Confidential Compute Architecture runs workloads in a hardware-isolated area restricting visibility to privileged software and other tenants, with attestation letting data owners verify their data is processed by intended software.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Arm CCA is a named commercial mechanism for confidential multi-tenant AI processing — a positioning advantage in secure AI infrastructure.

insights.claim: Secure storage now includes PUFs, key wrapping, and storing keys in encrypted scrambled states, and devices must be made post-quantum ready because quantum threats may arrive much sooner than expected while adding PQC late causes major problems.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Post-quantum readiness is a design-now imperative; late PQC retrofits are costly, favoring vendors with PQC-ready secure storage.
insights.quote: making sure that your device is post-quantum ready, because quantum days are likely here much sooner than we think
insights.locator: Databases and keys need to be encrypted

insights.claim: No single security measure suffices; AI data fusion requires combining hardware isolation with data provenance/integrity, access controls, secure software supply chains, and protected communications.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Defense-in-depth is the required posture — point solutions won't win; integrated security stacks are the value proposition.
insights.quote: No single security measure is sufficient by itself.
insights.locator: No single security measure is sufficient

insights.claim: Zonal architectures consolidating multiple sensor and software workloads onto shared compute make AI data fusion standard in vehicles but increase the car's attack surface, driving isolation of safety-critical from non-safety workloads.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Workload isolation is the emerging architectural trend as auto compute centralizes — a design requirement for automotive SoC vendors.
insights.quote: It is a key component of next-generation vehicle autonomy, but it also increases the car’s attack surface

insights.claim: Imagination uses hardware virtualization assigning each VM a dedicated hardware interface and OSID-tagged memory transactions enforced via the system MMU, with QoS and deadline-based preemption to protect against denial-of-service workloads.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Concrete hardware-virtualization mechanism for freedom-from-interference is a differentiator for safety-critical GPU IP.
insights.quote: It assigns each VM a dedicated hardware interface and OSID-tagged memory transactions, enforced via the system MMU

insights.claim: An automotive multi-camera image-analysis project required an HSM to secure not just finite-size boot images but 1 gigabit-per-second streaming IPsec traffic under ASIL B, where losing packets or desynchronization could make a car lag behind its true position.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Securing high-speed streaming data at line rate without dropping packets is a hard, safety-critical design constraint distinct from boot security.
insights.quote: if you are a little bit desynchronized, and your car is lagging with where it is compared to what it thinks it is, that is scary

insights.claim: Securing AI data fusion is a system-level, data-flow-oriented problem requiring worst-case design for 1 gigabit-per-second traffic with small packets and per-packet key rolling, maintaining decapsulation and integrity verification without stalling or dropping packets.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Bandwidth/compute headroom for continuous crypto checks at worst-case load is a hard silicon spec — security sits on the critical path in automotive.
insights.quote: in automotive, when you design a chip, you must design it for the worst case, and security is on the critical path
insights.locator: It’s not a question of chips

insights.claim: The intersection of AI and cybersecurity is the data itself, reducible to a simple rule: check data in and authenticate data out.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: A clean mental model for AI security investment: value accrues to input verification and output attestation technologies.
insights.quote: the intersection of cybersecurity and AI is data. And what we need to have in place is very simple — check data in and authenticate data out.

insights.claim: Concentrating sensitive information via data fusion expands attack surfaces such that one weak point could expose or corrupt the entire intelligence pool as data types, owners, and systems multiply.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Fusion concentration risk means aggregation itself is a liability — a driver for minimization and anonymization mandates.
insights.quote: the greater the risk that one weak point could expose or corrupt the broader intelligence pool
insights.locator: AI data fusion combines data from multiple sources

insights.claim: As agentic AI workloads increasingly combine data from multiple sources, building confidence in both the processing environment and the data itself becomes essential, with chip-level security as the critical foundation.
insights.speaker: Ann Mutschler
insights.so_what: Agentic AI raises the security bar further, extending demand for hardware-backed trust into autonomous multi-source workflows.
insights.locator: Looking ahead, as agentic AI workloads
